title: MoneyPrinterTurbo Codebase Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the AI-powered short video generation platform — covering architecture, services, API layer, web UI, and configuration

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: MoneyPrinterTurbo is an AI-powered platform that automatically generates short videos from a topic or keyword. It orchestrates large language models for scriptwriting, stock footage APIs for visuals, text-to-speech engines for voice-over, and video processing libraries for final assembly. The codebase follows an MVC plus service-layer pattern — the highlighted areas show the two entry points and the three core directories where backend logic, the web interface, and static resources live.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the application directory to see where all backend logic lives

steps.title: Application Entry Points
steps.description: The backend server launches on port 8080 via Uvicorn. The highlighted ASGI module wires up CORS, static file serving, and the API router. A separate Streamlit application serves as the alternative entry point, providing a user-friendly web interface for video generation.

interactiveActions.description: Click the ASGI module to see how the FastAPI app is configured

steps.title: Services — The Core Engine
steps.description:
This directory is the heart of the application. Each service handles one stage of the video generation pipeline:

• LLM integration — supports 13+ providers (OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, and more) for script and search-term generation
• Voice synthesis — text-to-speech via Edge TTS, Azure Speech, Gemini TTS, and SiliconFlow
• Subtitle generation — powered by Faster-Whisper or Edge TTS
• Material sourcing — stock footage from Pexels and Pixabay
• Video composition — final assembly with concatenation, subtitles, audio mixing, and transitions
• Task orchestration — coordinates the full pipeline from script through finished video
• State persistence — tracks task progress in memory or Redis

interactiveActions.description: Click the task orchestrator to see how the full video generation pipeline is coordinated

steps.title: Controllers — API Endpoints
steps.description:
This directory defines the REST API. The highlighted endpoint group handles video generation, task queries, material uploads, and background music management, along with script generation and search-term extraction.

The task management layer (highlighted separately) provides both in-memory and Redis-backed queues with concurrency control, preventing resource exhaustion under load.

interactiveActions.description: Click the video endpoint module to see the main API routes

steps.title: Models — Data Schemas & Constants
steps.description:
This directory defines the data structures used throughout the application:

• Schema definitions — Pydantic models for API validation covering video parameters, aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1), concatenation and transition modes, material metadata, and request/response contracts
• Constants — task states, supported file types, and text-processing punctuation
• Exceptions — custom error classes for HTTP responses and missing files

interactiveActions.description: Click the schema module to see the Pydantic models that define the API contract

steps.title: Web UI — Streamlit Interface
steps.description: This directory contains a Streamlit-based web application that provides a user-friendly alternative to the REST API. The highlighted entry point is a feature-rich single-page app with form inputs for video parameters, real-time progress tracking, video preview, and an editable configuration panel. The internationalization layer supports 7 languages: Chinese, English, German, Portuguese, Russian, Turkish, and Vietnamese.

interactiveActions.description: Click the web UI entry point to explore the Streamlit interface

steps.title: Configuration & Resources
steps.description:
Configuration is managed via TOML files. The highlighted template covers LLM provider keys, video source APIs, subtitle settings, Redis, proxy, and UI options. A dedicated config module loads and manages the runtime configuration.

The static resources directory holds fonts for subtitle rendering and copyright-free background music tracks. Deployment files for Docker and dependency management are also highlighted.

interactiveActions.description: Click the configuration template to see all available options

steps.title: The Complete Picture
steps.description:
You've now seen the full MoneyPrinterTurbo architecture:

1. **Entry Points** — the FastAPI backend and the Streamlit web interface
2. **Controllers** — REST API endpoints for video generation, scripts, and materials
3. **Services** — pipeline stages: LLM, materials, voice, subtitles, and video composition
4. **Models** — Pydantic schemas defining the API contract
5. **Task Management** — queue-based execution with in-memory or Redis backends
6. **Web UI** — a full-featured Streamlit app with internationalization
7. **Config & Resources** — TOML configuration, fonts, and background music

The video generation pipeline flows: Topic → Script → Search Terms → Stock Footage → Voice-over → Subtitles → Final Video.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the codebase freely — click on any building to view its source
